title: betula alba var. pubescens
intro: Betula alba var. pubescens, also known as downy birch, is a variety of the white birch tree that is characterized by its hairy, downy bark and slender branches. The bark of the downy birch tree has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal skin irritations and wounds. The medicinal properties of downy birch are attributed to its high content of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, and promote overall health and well-being. In herbal preparations, the bark of the downy birch tree can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from skin conditions to digestive problems.
